input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues here:

**1. `StaticResource` vs `DynamicResource`**

`StaticResource` resolves **once** at load time and never updates. For runtime theme switching, you must use `DynamicResource`:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Won't update at runtime -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✅ Updates when the resource dictionary changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes by replacing the merged dictionary at runtime:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme()); // or LightTheme
```

**2. App restarting on Android system theme change**

Android destroys and recreates the `Activity` on configuration changes like `uiMode` (dark/light). To prevent this, add `ConfigChanges` to your `MainActivity`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Orientation
        | ConfigChanges.UiMode       // ← handles dark mode without restart
        | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
        | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

With `ConfigChanges.UiMode`, Android won't recreate the activity. You can then detect the change and swap your `DynamicResource` theme programmatically by overriding `OnConfigurationChanged` or listening to `Application.RequestedThemeChanged`.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two issues with dark mode in a .NET MAUI Android app:

1. Using `StaticResource` instead of `DynamicResource` for theme switching at runtime
2. The app restarting when Android system dark mode changes

Let me answer directly — these are well-known .NET MAUI concepts, no codebase exploration needed.
